\index{bar construction!as Koszul dual coalgebra|textbf}
\index{Koszul dual!coalgebra vs.\ algebra|textbf}
The bar construction $\barB_X(\cA)$ of an augmented chiral
algebra~$\cA$ is a dg factorization \emph{coalgebra} on
$\operatorname{Ran}(X)$, the $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant quotient of the ordered bar $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA) = T^c(s^{-1}\bar\cA)$. Throughout this chapter, the unqualified notation $\barB_X(\cA)$ refers to this symmetric factorization bar on unordered $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$; the ordered tensor coalgebra is always written $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA)$. It is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}
of~$\cA$: the bar complex is not a tool for computing the dual,
it \emph{is} the dual.

When $\cA$ is Koszul, the bar coalgebra has cohomology concentrated
in bar degree~$1$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is a
separate object: it has generators $\mathcal{V}^\vee =
\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{V}, \omega_X)$
($\mathcal{D}_X$-module dual of the generators of~$\cA$) and
relations~$R^\perp$ (orthogonal complement under the residue
pairing). In the classical setting (algebras on a point),
$\cA^!$ is the graded linear dual of the concentrated bar
coalgebra. In the chiral setting, $\cA^!$ is characterized by
Theorem~A (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}):
Verdier duality on $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$ sends the bar
\emph{coalgebra} to the homotopy Koszul dual \emph{algebra},
 succeeded in 52ms:
 The derived center of the completed enveloping algebra is
 \[
 Z_{\mathrm{der}}(\widehat{\fg}_{-h^\vee})
 \;:=\;
 RHH^0_{\mathrm{ch}}(\widehat{\fg}_{-h^\vee})
 \]
 and the bar complex $\barB(\widehat{\fg}_{-h^\vee})$ is a
 concrete chain complex whose cohomology recovers the oper
 differential forms above.
 This supplies the manuscript's internal bridge to the oper side of
 the critical geometric Langlands picture; any stronger
 automorphic/spectral categorical identification remains external to
 the theorem proved here.
\end{enumerate}
\end{theorem}

The parallel is exact along every axis:
\begin{center}

At the $\mathcal{W}$-algebra level, the conjecture
$\mathcal{W}^k(\fg, f)^! \simeq \mathcal{W}^{k'}(\fg, f^D)$
translates through the master square to the geometric statement
that the \emph{Koszul dual of the bar complex on $J_\infty(S_f)$

$k \mapsto k'$ encoding the change in the Poisson structure.

 succeeded in 51ms:
For the $E_8$ root lattice, the bar complex $\barBgeom(\Vlat_{E_8})$ is
self-dual under Koszul duality (Theorem~\ref{thm:lattice:koszul-dual}),
reflecting the unimodularity and self-duality of $E_8$.
The bar complex decomposes as
\[
\barBgeom(\Vlat_{E_8})
= \bigoplus_{\gamma \in E_8} \barBgeom(\Vlat_{E_8})_\gamma
\]
with $|E_8/2E_8| = 2^8 = 256$ sectors modulo~$2E_8$. The 240 roots of
$E_8$ each contribute a 1-dimensional summand in bar degree~1.
\end{proposition}

\begin{proof}
\emph{Step~1.} Lattice decomposition.
The bar complex decomposes along the $E_8$-grading:
\[
\barBgeom(\Vlat_{E_8}) = \bigoplus_{\gamma \in E_8} \barBgeom(\Vlat_{E_8})_\gamma.

 succeeded in 51ms:

Q\psi^{(k)} = (k+1)\psi^{(k+1)} + \text{curvature terms}

compatible with the BRST differential on the $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ side.

\emph{Step~5.}
The residue pairing matrix of Step~3 is rank 3 (matching the 3 generators $\psi^{(-1)}, \psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(1)}$ paired against the 4 generators $\beta, \gamma, b, c$ of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system). The pairing $\langle \psi^{(i)}, - \rangle: \mathcal{B}^{\bullet} \to \mathbb{C}$ identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ with the linear dual of the cogenerators of $\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})$: the bar complex of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system has cogenerators in bar degree 1 dual to $\psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(\pm 1)}$, with the bar differential $d_{\bar{B}}$ encoding the BRST differential $Q$ of Step~4. The $A_\infty$ structure on $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ is then recovered as the Verdier dual $D_{\mathrm{Ran}}(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \bar{B}((\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!)$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}), identifying $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ as the homotopy Koszul dual $(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!_\infty$. Note: the cobar functor $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ (bar-cobar inversion, Theorem~B) recovers $\mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ itself, not its Koszul dual.
\end{evidence}


\begin{proof}
Apply Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-module-koszul-duality} (module Koszul duality) to
$\mathcal{A} = \mathcal{H}_k$. The hypotheses are satisfied: $\mathcal{H}_k$
is Koszul (the bar spectral sequence collapses at $E_2$ by the computation above), and
the Koszul dual coalgebra is $\bar{B}^{\mathrm{ch}}(\mathcal{H}_k)$ whose
graded linear dual is $\mathcal{H}_k^! \simeq \mathrm{Sym}^{\mathrm{ch}}(V^*)$ by
Theorem~\ref{thm:heisenberg-koszul-dual-early}. The passage from comodules to
modules over the dual algebra uses the finite-type grading of $\mathcal{H}_k$
(finite-dimensional weight spaces ensure graded dualizability).
\end{proof}
 succeeded in 51ms:
\section{The complementarity theorem}
\label{sec:complementarity-theorem}

Classical Koszul duality over a point is a discrete correspondence:
$\cA$ and~$\cA^!$ are linked by $\Omega \circ B$ and nothing more.
Already at genus~$0$ on a curve, the Fulton--MacPherson
compactifications $\overline{C}_n(X)$ and the Arnold forms
$\eta_{ij} = d\log(z_i - z_j)$ supply configuration-space geometry
that has no counterpart over a point: the collision residues that
define the bar differential, the averaging map
$\mathrm{av}\colon r(z) \mapsto \kappa$ that extracts the modular
characteristic from the spectral $r$-matrix, and the higher
operations $m_n$ for $n \geq 3$ that determine whether the shadow
tower terminates. At genus~$g \geq 1$, the fiberwise curvature
$d_{\mathrm{fib}}^2 = \kappa(\cA) \cdot \omega_g$ forces the two
algebras to interact through the moduli
$\overline{\mathcal{M}}_g$. The genus-$g$ obstruction space
decomposes into two complementary halves via Verdier duality:
Since every $E_\infty$-chiral algebra is in particular an $E_1$-chiral algebra, and the Koszul property at the $E_\infty$ level implies Koszulity at the $E_1$ level (the forgetful functor is exact on bar complexes), we may apply the module Koszul duality equivalence
(Theorem~\ref{thm:e1-module-koszul-duality}), which gives:

$\mathrm{Vir}_{26-c}$ rather than an H-level infinite-generator dual
object; W-algebras ($N \geq 3$) involve cubic and higher-order OPE

For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.
\end{observation}

 succeeded in 51ms:
\label{rem:ordered-verdier-nonexistence}
\index{Verdier duality!ordered bar obstruction}
The Verdier intertwining
$\mathbb{D}_{\mathrm{Ran}}\barB^{\Sigma}(\cA) \simeq \barB^{\Sigma}(\cA^!)$
(Theorem~A, Convention~\textup{\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}})
is a statement about the \emph{symmetric} bar~$\barB^{\Sigma}$
on~$\mathrm{Ran}(X)$. Here $\cA^!$ is the Verdier-dual algebra.
A naive ordered analogue
$\mathbb{D}_{\mathrm{Ran}}(\barB^{\mathrm{ord}}(\cA))
\simeq \barB^{\mathrm{ord}}(\cA^!)$
does \emph{not} exist: the ordered bar lives on ordered
configurations $\mathrm{Conf}_n^{<}(X)$, not on~$\mathrm{Ran}(X)$,
and pushing forward to~$\mathrm{Ran}$ via the
carry an $\mathsf{E}_3$ structure: a curve provides only~$\bR^2$,
and the passage to $\mathsf{E}_3$ requires $\bR^3$ geometry. The
correct statement is that, for $\Einf$-chiral algebras
(where $\barB^{\Sigma}(\cA)$ exists), the \emph{derived center}
$Z^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA)
= \HH^*(\barB^{\Sigma}(\cA), \barB^{\Sigma}(\cA))$
carries $\mathsf{E}_3$ structure by the Higher Deligne Conjecture,

index 13bfe18bd6d9f8d05071f80f991bdf0599603aa8..7d1aa474d180f38d6f7b0e5557155b88a935fbe2
--- a/chapters/theory/derived_langlands.tex

@@ -125,9 +125,10 @@
  \;:=\;
  RHH^0_{\mathrm{ch}}(\widehat{\fg}_{-h^\vee})
  \]
- and the bar complex $\barB(\widehat{\fg}_{-h^\vee})$ is a
- concrete chain complex whose cohomology recovers the oper
- differential forms above.
+ and is computed in this chapter using the critical bar complex
+ $\barB(\widehat{\fg}_{-h^\vee})$ as a resolution input. Separately,
+ the cohomology of that bar complex recovers the oper differential
+ forms above.
  This supplies the manuscript's internal bridge to the oper side of
  the critical geometric Langlands picture; any stronger
  automorphic/spectral categorical identification remains external to
@@ -149,9 +150,10 @@
 together with formal smoothness of the oper space
 (Frenkel--Gaitsgory~\cite{FG06}). Part~(iii) is the internal
 bookkeeping statement that the derived center
-$RHH^0_{\mathrm{ch}}(\widehat{\fg}_{-h^\vee})$ is represented here by
-the critical bar complex, whose cohomology recovers the oper
-differential-form package. This uses the bar-Ext identification
+$RHH^0_{\mathrm{ch}}(\widehat{\fg}_{-h^\vee})$ is computed here via
+the critical bar complex as a bar-Ext resolution; the bar complex
+itself remains distinct from the derived center, and its cohomology
+recovers the oper differential-form package. This uses the bar-Ext identification
 together with the Frenkel--Teleman theorem~\cite{FT06}; it does
 not identify the bar complex with the derived global sections of the
 oper structure sheaf.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '2878,2888p' chapters/theory/higher_genus_complementarity.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
\end{equation}
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
Via bar construction and duality:
\begin{enumerate}
\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
\item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
\item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
\end{enumerate}

 algebra~$\cA$ is a dg factorization \emph{coalgebra} on
 $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$, the $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant quotient of the ordered bar $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA) = T^c(s^{-1}\bar\cA)$. Throughout this chapter, the unqualified notation $\barB_X(\cA)$ refers to this symmetric factorization bar on unordered $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$; the ordered tensor coalgebra is always written $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA)$. It is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}
-of~$\cA$: the bar complex is not a tool for computing the dual,
-it \emph{is} the dual.
+of~$\cA$: the bar complex is the bar coalgebra that computes the dual
+coalgebra
+\[
+\cA^i \;:=\; H^*(\barB_X(\cA)).
+\]
+Thus $\barB_X(\cA)$, $\cA^i$, and $\cA^!$ are distinct objects.
 
 When $\cA$ is Koszul, the bar coalgebra has cohomology concentrated
-in bar degree~$1$. The Koszul dual \emph{algebra}~$\cA^!$ is a
+in bar degree~$1$, so $\cA^i$ is concentrated there. The Koszul dual
+\emph{algebra}~$\cA^! = ((\cA^i)^\vee)$ is a
 separate object: it has generators $\mathcal{V}^\vee =
 \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{V}, \omega_X)$
 ($\mathcal{D}_X$-module dual of the generators of~$\cA$) and
 relations~$R^\perp$ (orthogonal complement under the residue
 pairing). In the classical setting (algebras on a point),
-$\cA^!$ is the graded linear dual of the concentrated bar
-coalgebra. In the chiral setting, $\cA^!$ is characterized by
+$\cA^!$ is the graded linear dual of the concentrated dual
+coalgebra~$\cA^i$. In the chiral setting, $\cA^!$ is characterized by
 Theorem~A (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}):
 Verdier duality on $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$ sends the bar
 \emph{coalgebra} to the homotopy Koszul dual \emph{algebra},

 together with formal smoothness of the oper space

 not identify the bar complex with the derived global sections of the
 oper structure sheaf.

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]

+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

file update
M /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar/chapters/theory/higher_genus_complementarity.tex
@@ -2883,4 +2883,4 @@
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.

 algebra~$\cA$ is a dg factorization \emph{coalgebra} on
 $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$, the $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant quotient of the ordered bar $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA) = T^c(s^{-1}\bar\cA)$.  \section{The complementarity theorem}

@@ -2878,8 +2881,8 @@
 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}
@@ -4061,7 +4064,9 @@
 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '980,990p' chapters/examples/lattice_foundations.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 52ms:
involves the product $\mu$ composed with Koszul signs from the
desuspension. For the lattice algebra, where $\mu(e^\alpha \otimes
e^\beta) = \varepsilon(\alpha,\beta)\,e^{\alpha+\beta}$ with
$|\varepsilon(\alpha,\beta)| = \pm 1$, the desuspension introduces a
sign $(-1)^{|\alpha||\beta|}$ that, combined with the bilinearity of
$\varepsilon$, produces the cocycle inversion:
the bar-complex coproduct has structure constants
$\varepsilon(\alpha,\beta)^{-1}$.
Since $\varepsilon^{-1}$ is again a valid cocycle,
$(\Vlat_\Lambda^{\varepsilon^{-1}})^c$ is a valid coalgebra, and the
Koszul duality exchanges the two.

 $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$, the $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant quotient of the ordered bar $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA) = T^c(s^{-1}\bar\cA)$. Throughout this chapter, the unqualified notation $\barB_X(\cA)$ refers to this symmetric factorization bar on unordered $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$; the ordered tensor coalgebra is always written $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA)$. It is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}

-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

 \emph{Step~5.}
-The residue pairing matrix of Step~3 is rank 3 (matching the 3 generators $\psi^{(-1)}, \psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(1)}$ paired against the 4 generators $\beta, \gamma, b, c$ of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system). The pairing $\langle \psi^{(i)}, - \rangle: \mathcal{B}^{\bullet} \to \mathbb{C}$ identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ with the linear dual of the cogenerators of $\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})$: the bar complex of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system has cogenerators in bar degree 1 dual to $\psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(\pm 1)}$, with the bar differential $d_{\bar{B}}$ encoding the BRST differential $Q$ of Step~4. The $A_\infty$ structure on $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ is then recovered as the Verdier dual $D_{\mathrm{Ran}}(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \bar{B}((\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!)$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}), identifying $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ as the homotopy Koszul dual $(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!_\infty$. This identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ as the homotopy Koszul dual $(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!_\infty$. Note: the cobar functor $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ (bar-cobar inversion, Theorem~B) recovers $\mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ itself, not its Koszul dual.

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.

+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}
 \index{model independence}

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.

 Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-functorial-complete}.

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}

@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:

-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).

+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

-For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.
+For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra. Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.

+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

 relations; and the affine Yangian has relations involving spectral

Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.

The pairing $\langle \psi^{(i)}, - \rangle: \mathcal{B}^{\bullet} \to \mathbb{C}$ identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ with the linear dual of the cogenerators of $\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})$: the bar complex of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system has cogenerators in bar degree 1 dual to $\psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(\pm 1)}$, with the bar differential $d_{\bar{B}}$ encoding the BRST differential $Q$ of Step~4. The $A_\infty$ structure on $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ is then recovered as the Verdier dual $D_{\mathrm{Ran}}(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \bar{B}((\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!)$ (Theorem~\ref{thm:quantum-complementarity-main}), identifying $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ as the homotopy Koszul dual $(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!_\infty$. Note: the cobar functor $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ (bar-cobar inversion, Theorem~B) recovers $\mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ itself, not its Koszul dual.
+The residue pairing matrix of Step~3 is rank 3 (matching the 3 generators $\psi^{(-1)}, \psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(1)}$ paired against the 4 generators $\beta, \gamma, b, c$ of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system). The pairing $\langle \psi^{(i)}, - \rangle: \mathcal{B}^{\bullet} \to \mathbb{C}$ identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ with the linear dual of the cogenerators of $\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})$: the bar complex of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system has cogenerators in bar degree 1 dual to $\psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(\pm 1)}$, with the bar differential $d_{\bar{B}}$ encoding the BRST differential $Q$ of Step~4. The $A_\infty$ structure on $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ is then recovered from the Verdier dual $\mathbb{D}_{\mathrm{Ran}}(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet}))$, which is the homotopy Koszul-dual factorization algebra computed from the bar resolution (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}). This identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ as the homotopy Koszul dual $(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!_\infty$. Note: the cobar functor $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ (bar-cobar inversion, Theorem~B) recovers $\mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ itself, not its Koszul dual.
 \end{evidence}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}

 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

 relations; and the affine Yangian has relations involving spectral

-For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.
+For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra. Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}
 \index{model independence}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

 relations; and the affine Yangian has relations involving spectral

-For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.

Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}

/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '6780,6788p' chapters/theory/en_koszul_duality.tex" in /Users/raeez/chiral-bar-cobar succeeded in 51ms:
\textbf{Part~(iv).}
The Higher Deligne Conjecture states that for an $\En$-algebra~$A$,
the Hochschild cohomology $\HH^*(A, A)$ carries a natural
$\mathsf{E}_{n+1}$ structure. At $n = 2$: if
$\barB^{\Sigma}(\cA)$ is $\Etwo$, then
$\HH^*(\barB^{\Sigma}(\cA), \barB^{\Sigma}(\cA))$ is $\mathsf{E}_3$.
Applied to the symmetric bar resolution of~$\cA$, this yields the
$\mathsf{E}_3$ structure on the derived center
$Z^{\mathrm{der}}_{\mathrm{ch}}(\cA) = \HH^*(\cA,\cA)$.

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}
 \index{model independence}

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.

+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}

-For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.
+For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra. Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}
 \index{model independence}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

 relations; and the affine Yangian has relations involving spectral

Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}
 \index{model independence}

Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.

+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.

This identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ as the homotopy Koszul dual $(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!_\infty$. Note: the cobar functor $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ (bar-cobar inversion, Theorem~B) recovers $\mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ itself, not its Koszul dual.

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}

 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+

 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

@@ -4061,7 +4064,9 @@
 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

-For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.
+For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra. Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}
 
 \subsection{The completion solution}

chapters/theory/introduction.tex:26:is always a dg algebra morphism. When it is a quasi-isomorphism, $A$ is \emph{Koszul}: the bar cohomology concentrates in degree~$1$, the Koszul dual coalgebra is $A^{\scriptstyle \text{\normalfont !\textasciigrave}} = H^1(B(A))$, and the Koszul dual algebra is $A^! = (A^{\scriptstyle \text{\normalfont !\textasciigrave}})^\vee = T(V^*)/R^\perp$. The derived categories of $A$-modules and $A^!$-modules become equivalent (Priddy~\cite{Priddy70}, Beilinson--Ginzburg--Soergel~\cite{BGS96}, Loday--Vallette~\cite{LV12}). The theory is complete: $\Omega(B(A)) \simeq A$ is bar-cobar \emph{inversion}, and nothing beyond this equivalence remains.

 algebra~$\cA$ is a dg factorization \emph{coalgebra} on
 $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$, the $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant quotient of the ordered bar $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA) = T^c(s^{-1}\bar\cA)$. Throughout this chapter, the unqualified notation $\barB_X(\cA)$ refers to this symmetric factorization bar on unordered $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$; the ordered tensor coalgebra is always written $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA)$. +coalgebra

+\]
+Thus $\barB_X(\cA)$, $\cA^i$, and $\cA^!$ are distinct objects.

 pairing). In the classical setting (algebras on a point),

+$\cA^!$ is the graded linear dual of the concentrated dual

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}

 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]

+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}

 relations; and the affine Yangian has relations involving spectral

-For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.
+For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra. Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}

The bar construction $\barB_X(\cA)$ of an augmented chiral
algebra~$\cA$ is a dg factorization \emph{coalgebra} on
$\operatorname{Ran}(X)$, the $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant quotient of the ordered bar $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA) = T^c(s^{-1}\bar\cA)$. Throughout this chapter, the unqualified notation $\barB_X(\cA)$ refers to this symmetric factorization bar on unordered $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$; the ordered tensor coalgebra is always written $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA)$. It is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}
of~$\cA$: the bar complex is the bar coalgebra that computes the dual
coalgebra
\[
\cA^i \;:=\; H^*(\barB_X(\cA)).

The pairing $\langle \psi^{(i)}, - \rangle: \mathcal{B}^{\bullet} \to \mathbb{C}$ identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ with the linear dual of the cogenerators of $\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})$: the bar complex of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system has cogenerators in bar degree 1 dual to $\psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(\pm 1)}$, with the bar differential $d_{\bar{B}}$ encoding the BRST differential $Q$ of Step~4. The pairing $\langle \psi^{(i)}, - \rangle: \mathcal{B}^{\bullet} \to \mathbb{C}$ identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ with the linear dual of the cogenerators of $\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})$: the bar complex of the derived $\beta\gamma$-$bc$ system has cogenerators in bar degree 1 dual to $\psi^{(0)}, \psi^{(\pm 1)}$, with the bar differential $d_{\bar{B}}$ encoding the BRST differential $Q$ of Step~4. The $A_\infty$ structure on $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ is then recovered from the Verdier dual $\mathbb{D}_{\mathrm{Ran}}(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet}))$, which is the homotopy Koszul-dual factorization algebra computed from the bar resolution (Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main}). This identifies $\mathcal{F}^{\bullet}$ as the homotopy Koszul dual $(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})^!_\infty$. Note: the cobar functor $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{B}^{\bullet})) \simeq \mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ (bar-cobar inversion, Theorem~B) recovers $\mathcal{B}^{\bullet}$ itself, not its Koszul dual.

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}
 \index{model independence}

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

 relations; and the affine Yangian has relations involving spectral

-For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.
+For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra. Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}

+\begin{proof}
+The first sentence is the square-zero statement already proved for the
+total bar differential. If
+$f \colon \cA \to \cA'$ is a morphism of chiral algebras, applying~$f$
+to each algebra factor commutes with the internal differential, with
+the collision residues, and with the de~Rham differential, so
+$\bar B^{\mathrm{geom}}(f)$ is a chain map. Compatibility with the
+coproduct, identities, and composition is checked on the same tensor
+factors, yielding the stated functor.
+\end{proof}
+
 \subsection{Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces}
 
 \begin{theorem}[Stokes' theorem on configuration spaces; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{thm:stokes-config}
@@ -1469,6 +1487,15 @@
 A commutative diagram of chiral algebras induces a commutative diagram of bar complexes: if $k \circ f = g \circ h$ in $\mathsf{ChirAlg}_X$, then $\bar{B}(k) \circ \bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g) \circ \bar{B}(h)$ in $\mathsf{dgCoalg}_X$. This is immediate from functoriality.
 \end{corollary}
 
+\begin{proof}
+By Corollary~\ref{cor:bar-functorial}, the bar construction is a
+functor. Applying that functor to the equality
+$k \circ f = g \circ h$ gives
+$\bar{B}(k \circ f) = \bar{B}(g \circ h)$, and functoriality expands
+this as
+$\bar{B}(k)\circ\bar{B}(f) = \bar{B}(g)\circ\bar{B}(h)$.
+\end{proof}
+
 \begin{proposition}[Model independence; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]
 \label{prop:model-independence}
 \index{model independence}

 \begin{proof}
 Via bar construction and duality:
 \begin{enumerate}
-\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (exterior coalgebra, the Koszul dual coalgebra).
-\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! = \bar{B}(\mathcal{F})^\vee \cong \beta\gamma$ (linear dual of the bar coalgebra). Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
+\item Bar complex: $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F}) = \Lambda^{*,c}(\psi^*, \partial\psi^*, \ldots)$ (the exterior bar coalgebra on the desuspended generators).
+\item Koszul dual algebra: $\mathcal{F}^! \cong \beta\gamma$ is obtained by dualizing the dual coalgebra computed from $\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})$; in this free case that amounts to the linear dual of the bar coalgebra. Bar-cobar inversion gives $\Omega(\bar{B}(\mathcal{F})) \cong \mathcal{F}$ (recovers the fermion, not $\beta\gamma$).
 \item Conversely: $\bar{B}(\beta\gamma)$ has nonzero cohomology classes $[\beta \otimes \beta], [\gamma \otimes \gamma] \in H^2(\bar{B}(\beta\gamma))$, which dualize to the fermionic relation $\psi^2 = 0$.
 \item Koszul dual: $(\beta\gamma)^! = \bar{B}(\beta\gamma)^\vee \cong \mathcal{F}$.
 \end{enumerate}

 \begin{lemma}[AF duality for chiral algebras; \ClaimStatusProvedHere]\label{lem:AF-dual-chiral}
 The Ayala--Francis duality for a chiral algebra $\mathcal{A}$ is:
 \[\int_{\text{Conf}_n(X)} \mathcal{A} \simeq \mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}\left(\int_{-\text{Conf}_n(X)} \bar{B}(\mathcal{A})\right)\]
-where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra (Koszul dual) and $\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
+where $\bar{B}(\mathcal{A})$ is the bar coalgebra computing the dual
+coalgebra $\mathcal{A}^i = H^*(\barB(\mathcal{A}))$, and
+$\mathbb{D}_{\text{top}}$ is topological (linear) duality.
 \end{lemma}
 
 \begin{proof}[Proof of Lemma]

 relations; and the affine Yangian has relations involving spectral

-For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra (it \emph{is} the Koszul dual coalgebra in the derived sense, Convention~\ref{conv:bar-coalgebra-identity}), but its cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ recovers $\cA^!$ as a graded piece of a filtered inverse limit.
+For these, the bar construction $\bar{B}(\cA)$ remains a perfectly well-defined dg coalgebra. Its cohomology coalgebra $H^*(\barB(\cA))$ is the dual coalgebra when that cohomology concentrates, but in the non-quadratic setting the cohomology does not concentrate in a single degree, so the classical Koszul dual algebra $\cA^!$ is not directly visible. $I$-adic completion resolves this: the completed bar coalgebra $\widehat{\bar{B}}(\cA)$ organizes the filtered data from which $\cA^!$ is recovered.
 \end{observation}

The bar construction $\barB_X(\cA)$ of an augmented chiral
algebra~$\cA$ is a dg factorization \emph{coalgebra} on
$\operatorname{Ran}(X)$, the $\Sigma_n$-coinvariant quotient of the ordered bar $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA) = T^c(s^{-1}\bar\cA)$. Throughout this chapter, the unqualified notation $\barB_X(\cA)$ refers to this symmetric factorization bar on unordered $\operatorname{Ran}(X)$; the ordered tensor coalgebra is always written $B^{\mathrm{ord}}_X(\cA)$. It is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}
of~$\cA$: the bar complex is the bar coalgebra that computes the dual
coalgebra
 succeeded in 51ms:

\item\label{D:dual}
A candidate Koszul dual chiral algebra $\cA^!$ on~$X$ with
universal twisting morphism
$\tau\colon \bar{B}_X(\cA) \to \cA^!$. The identification
$\bar{B}_X(\cA)^\vee \simeq \cA^!$ as factorization algebras is a
\emph{consequence} of Theorem~\ref{thm:bar-cobar-isomorphism-main},
not an input; $\bar{B}_X(\cA)$ itself is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}.

\item\label{D:genus-tower}
A genus tower $\{\bar{B}^{(g)}_X(\cA)\}_{g \geq 0}$ extending the
genus-$0$ bar construction to all genera via propagators on
$\overline{C}_n(\Sigma_g)$
 succeeded in 51ms:
chapters/theory/higher_genus_modular_koszul.tex:555:not an input; $\bar{B}_X(\cA)$ itself is the Koszul dual \emph{coalgebra}.
